qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Docker API output_path permits arbitrary server-side file write
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes the Docker API accepting `file://` URLs on `/execute_js`, `/screenshot`, `/pdf`, and `/html`, which lets an attacker read arbitrary local files. This finding instead uses `output_path` on `/screenshot` and `/pdf` to persist rendered bytes after `validate_url_scheme(body.url)` succeeds, so it is a different sink and a different filesystem bug.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding in this session identifies the advisory's `file://` URL acceptance and arbitrary-file-read root cause. The only qualifying finding is a separate arbitrary-write issue in the Docker API's `output_path` persistence branch.
evidence: The advisory JSON says `/execute_js`, `/screenshot`, `/pdf`, and `/html` accepted `file://` URLs and allowed arbitrary local file reads such as `file:///etc/passwd`.

The session's source-trace evidence records `validate_url_scheme()` as the advisory-relevant guard and marks the `file://`/URL-scheme family cleared, with release notes stating that v0.8.0 rejects `file://` URLs on those endpoints.

The only `qualifying_vuln` report describes `POST /screenshot` and `/pdf` reaching `os.path.abspath(body.output_path)` and `open(path, "wb")` after URL validation, and its PoC evidence shows a planted PNG served back from `/static/assets/evohunt-proof.png`, which is an arbitrary write rather than the advisory's arbitrary read.
